mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides best-practice fast paths for bootstrapping a secure and functional cluster. While it is user-friendly, it requires a solid understanding of Kubernetes concepts and may not cover all advanced configurations.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is essential for creating and managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb http://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

```

> Add Google Cloud public key for apt.
> Add Kubernetes apt repository.
> Update the package list.
> Install specific versions of kubelet, kubeadm, and kubectl.
> Prevent automatic updates of kubelet, kubeadm, and kubectl.
commands: echo 'deb http://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command bootstraps the control-plane node of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version=v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=192.168.0.0/16

```

> Initialize the cluster with a specific Kubernetes version and pod network CIDR.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig for the Admin User
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the newly created cluster by setting up the kubeconfig file.

```bash

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Create the kube directory.
> Copy the admin config to the user's kube directory.
> Change ownership of the kube config file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to enable communication between pods.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://docs.projectcalico.org/manifests/calico.yaml

```

> Install Calico as the network add-on.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify that the control-plane node is in 'Ready' state.

uninstall.title: Reset the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove all Kubernetes components and reset the cluster state. Data loss may occur, and orphaned resources may remain.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

sudo rm -rf $HOME/.kube

```

> Reset the kubeadm state.
> Remove kubeconfig directory.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster state and drain nodes if necessary. This step upgrades the cluster to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Check available upgrades.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet service may not be started or is failing to run.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo systemctl start kubelet' to start the kubelet service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm join failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The token used for joining may have expired or is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Generate a new token using 'kubeadm token create --print-join-command' and try again.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the API server is down.

**Fix:**
Check the kubeconfig file at '$HOME/.kube/config' and ensure the API server is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod network not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The network add-on may not be installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the network add-on using the appropriate manifest file.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new features like the PodSecurity admission controller and improved handling of network policies.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of --pod-network-cidr flag in favor of using specific network add-ons.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure that all network policies are reviewed for compatibility.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with your control-plane node listed as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: echo 'deb http://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00
sudo apt-mark hold kubelet kubeadm kubectl

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements for running Kubernetes.
